title: Rustia costaricensis
taxonomy: Rustia costaricensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family consists of over 13,000 known species, many of which are native to tropical regions around the world. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Rustia costaricensis is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes other flowering plant families like the gentian and loganiaceae families. The plant's classification is further broken down into the genus Rustia and the species R. costaricensis. Understanding the classification of Rustia costaricensis provides valuable insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
uses: Rustia costaricensis has been reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and other ailments. In some cultures, it is believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties. However, further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. Traditional healers in Central America have used Rustia costaricensis for centuries to treat various health conditions.

However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects on human health.
